post.description: Get Posts

Create posts for all supported platforms. It is possible to create customized posts per channel by using the same platform account IDs in a request and hitting the create post API multiple times with different summaries and account IDs per platform.

The content and media limitations, as well as platform rate limiters corresponding to the respective platforms, are provided in the following reference link:

  Link: [Platform Limitations](https://help.leadconnectorhq.com/support/solutions/articles/48001240003-social-planner-image-video-content-and-api-limitations "Social Planner Help")

put.description:
Create posts for all supported platforms. It is possible to create customized posts per channel by using the same platform account IDs in a request and hitting the create post API multiple times with different summaries and account IDs per platform.

The content and media limitations, as well as platform rate limiters corresponding to the respective platforms, are provided in the following reference link:

  Link: [Platform Limitations](https://help.leadconnectorhq.com/support/solutions/articles/48001240003-social-planner-image-video-content-and-api-limitations "Social Planner Help")

post.description:
Deletes multiple posts based on the provided list of post IDs. 
                  This operation is useful for clearing up large numbers of posts efficiently. 
                  
Note: 
                  
1.The maximum number of posts that can be deleted in a single request is '50'.
                  
2.However, It will only get deleted in CRM database but still
                   it is recommended to be cautious of this operation.

400.description: Cannot delete more than 50 posts at a time.

404.description: No posts found with the given IDs.

500.description: An error occurred while trying to delete the posts. Please try again later.

delete.description: Delete account and account from group

get.description:
## OAuth Connection Flow - Step 1: Initiate OAuth

This is the first step in the 3-step OAuth flow to connect a social media account:

1. **Start OAuth** (this endpoint) → User authenticates with the platform
2. **Get Accounts** → Retrieve available pages/channels to connect
3. **Attach Account** → Connect the selected account to your location

### How to Use

Open this API in a browser window (not via cURL) with the required query parameters. The user will be redirected to the platform's OAuth login screen.

### Receiving the OAuth Response

After successful authentication, the OAuth window will post a message back to your application. Listen for this message to get the `accountId` needed for the next step.

```javascript
window.addEventListener('message', function(e) {
  if (e.data && e.data.page === 'social_media_posting') {
    const { actionType, page, platform, placement, accountId, reconnectAccounts } = e.data;
    // Use accountId for Step 2: GET /oauth/{locationId}/{platform}/accounts/{accountId}
  }
}, false);
```

### Event Data Response

| Field | Type | Example | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| actionType | string | "close" | The action type |
| page | string | "social-media-posting" | Source page identifier |
| platform | string | "facebook" | The OAuth platform |
| placement | string | "placement" | Placement context |
| accountId | string | "658a9b6833b91e0ecb8f3958" | **Use this for Step 2** |
| reconnectAccounts | string[] | ["658a9b...", "efd2da..."] | Accounts that need reconnection |

### Next Step

Use the `accountId` from the response to call:
```
GET /social-media-posting/oauth/{locationId}/{platform}/accounts/{accountId}
```

### Platform Notes

- **bluesky**: Currently not supported, will return an error
- **tiktok-business**: Uses a separate business OAuth flow

parameters.name: platform
parameters.description:
Social media platform to connect. Each platform has specific account types:
- **google**: Google Business Profile locations
- **facebook**: Facebook Pages
- **instagram**: Instagram Professional Accounts (Business/Creator)
- **linkedin**: LinkedIn Pages and Profiles
- **tiktok**: TikTok Creator Accounts
- **tiktok-business**: TikTok Business Center Accounts
- **youtube**: YouTube Channels
- **pinterest**: Pinterest Business Accounts
- **threads**: Threads Profiles
- **bluesky**: Bluesky Accounts (currently not supported)

get.description:
## OAuth Connection Flow - Step 2: Get Available Accounts

After completing OAuth authentication (Step 1), use this endpoint to retrieve the list of available pages, channels, or locations that can be connected.

### OAuth Flow Position

1. **Start OAuth** → User authenticates, returns `accountId`
2. **Get Accounts** (this endpoint) → Lists available pages/channels to connect
3. **Attach Account** → Connect the selected account

### What This Returns

The response varies by platform:

| Platform | Returns |
|----------|--------|
| **facebook** | List of Facebook Pages the user manages |
| **instagram** | List of Instagram Professional Accounts (linked to Facebook Pages) |
| **google** | Google Business Profile locations |
| **linkedin** | LinkedIn Pages and Profile |
| **tiktok** | TikTok Creator account info |
| **tiktok-business** | TikTok Business Center accounts |
| **youtube** | YouTube Channels |
| **pinterest** | Pinterest Business accounts and boards |
| **threads** | Threads profiles |

### Next Step

From the response, select the account/page you want to connect and use its details in Step 3:
```
POST /social-media-posting/oauth/{locationId}/{platform}/accounts/{accountId}
```

parameters.name: accountId
parameters.description: The OAuth Account ID received from Step 1 (Start OAuth) via the window message event

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Search term to filter accounts/pages by name. Useful when the user has many pages to choose from.

200.description: Returns available accounts/pages/channels that can be connected. Response structure varies by platform - see examples below.

facebook.description: Returns Facebook Pages the user can manage. Use the page's `id` as `originId` when attaching.

instagram.description: Returns Instagram Professional Accounts (Business/Creator) linked to Facebook Pages.

google.description: Returns Google Business Profile locations. Use both `location` and `account` objects when attaching.

linkedin.description: Returns LinkedIn company pages and personal profile the user can post from.

youtube.description: Returns YouTube channels the user owns.

post.description:
## OAuth Connection Flow - Step 3: Connect the Account

This is the final step in the OAuth flow. After retrieving available accounts (Step 2), use this endpoint to connect the selected account to your location.

### OAuth Flow Summary

1. **Start OAuth** → User authenticates with platform
2. **Get Accounts** → Retrieved available pages/channels
3. **Attach Account** (this endpoint) → Connect the selected account

### Request Body by Platform

The request body structure varies depending on the platform:

#### Facebook / Instagram
```json
{
  "type": "page",
  "originId": "244405XXXXX11687",
  "name": "My Facebook Page",
  "avatar": "https://..." // optional
}
```

#### Google Business Profile
```json
{
  "location": {
    "name": "locations/12345",
    "title": "My Business Location",
    "storeCode": "STORE123",
    "isVerified": "ChIJsZQpj1qbXjkRQNDUG4UUx6k"
  },
  "account": {
    "name": "accounts/12345",
    "accountName": "My Business Account",
    "type": "LOCATION_GROUP",
    "verificationState": "VERIFIED",
    "vettedState": "VETTED"
  }
}
```

#### LinkedIn
```json
{
  "type": "page",
  "originId": "urn:li:organization:12345",
  "name": "My LinkedIn Page",
  "avatar": "https://..." // optional
}
```

#### TikTok
```json
{
  "originId": "7234567890123456789",
  "name": "My TikTok Account",
  "avatar": "https://..." // optional
}
```

#### YouTube
```json
{
  "originId": "UCxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx",
  "name": "My YouTube Channel",
  "avatar": "https://..." // optional
}
```

#### Pinterest
```json
{
  "originId": "123456789",
  "name": "My Pinterest Account",
  "avatar": "https://..." // optional
}
```

### After Connection

Once connected, the account will appear in your location's connected accounts and can be used for social media posting.

parameters.name: locationId
parameters.description: The Location ID where you want to connect this social account

parameters.name: platform
parameters.description: Social media platform (must match the platform used in Steps 1 and 2)

parameters.name: accountId
parameters.description: The OAuth Account ID received from Step 1 (same as used in Step 2)

requestBody.description: Account details to connect. The structure varies by platform - see description above for examples.

201.description: Successful response - Account attached. Response structure varies by platform.

get.description: Retrieve all categories for a specific location with optional search and pagination
Retrieve a specific category by its ID
Retrieve all tags for a specific location with optional search and pagination

post.description: Retrieve specific tags by their IDs

Retrieve analytics data for multiple social media accounts. Supports custom date ranges for both the current period and a comparison period. If no date ranges are provided, defaults to the last 7 days (excluding today) with comparison to the previous 7 days.

profileIds.description: Array of connected social media account IDs to fetch analytics for. This can be found as 'profileId' in /accounts api.

platforms.description: Array of social media platforms to filter analytics by. If not provided, all platforms will be included. NOTE: Linkedin (PAGE only) and Tiktok (BUSINESS only) are supported.

currentRange.description: Custom date range for the current analytics period. If omitted, defaults to the last 7 days (excluding today) with automatic comparison to the previous 7 days.

endDate.description: End date in ISO 8601 format. Must be after startDate.

prevRange.description: Comparison date range. Can only be provided when currentRange is also present. If omitted while currentRange is present, no comparison data is returned.

endDate.description: End date in ISO 8601 format. Must be after startDate.

default-behavior.summary: Default behavior (backward compatible, no date ranges)
default-behavior.description: Returns last 7 days (excluding today) with comparison to the previous 7 days.

results.description: Analytics data grouped by metrics and platforms

dayRange.description: Array of day names for the analytics period

followers.description: Total number of followers across all platforms

impressions.description: Total number of impressions across all posts

posts.description: Number of posts published per day, organized by platform

impressions.description: Daily impressions over the analytics period

likes.description: Daily likes over the analytics period

comments.description: Daily comments over the analytics period

breakdowns.description: Detailed breakdowns by metric and platform

total.description: Total number of posts across all platforms

totalChange.description: Percentage change in total posts compared to previous period

platforms.description: Platform-specific post counts with change percentages

value.description: Number of posts for this platform

change.description: Percentage change compared to previous period

totalChange.description: Percentage change in total impressions compared to previous period

platforms.description: Platform-specific impression counts with change percentages

value.description: Number of impressions for this platform

change.description: Percentage change compared to previous period

totalChange.description: Percentage change in total reach compared to previous period

platforms.description: Platform-specific reach counts with change percentages

change.description: Percentage change compared to previous period

likes.description: Number of likes received on this platform

comments.description: Number of comments received on this platform

shares.description: Number of shares on this platform

change.description: Percentage change in engagement compared to previous period

platformTotals.description: Platform-wise totals with time series data

series.description: Daily impression counts over the analytics period
Daily follower counts over the analytics period
Daily like counts over the analytics period

totals.description: Total counts and percentages by gender

total.description: Total number of male audience members
Total number of female audience members
Total number of audience members with unknown gender

percentage.description: Percentage of audience with unknown gender

65+.description: Number of audience members aged 65 and above

message.description: Success message indicating the analytics were built successfully

traceId.description: Unique identifier for tracking this request

400.description: Bad Request - Occurs when: more than 100 accounts are requested, invalid date formats are provided, startDate is after endDate, prevRange is provided without currentRange, or invalid platform values are specified.

401.description: Unauthorized - Invalid or missing authentication credentials

422.description: Unprocessable Entity - Invalid request body format

get.summary: Get all categories with their queue status
get.description: Returns categories with status: "available" (no queue), "in_queue" (active/paused queue), or "draft" (queue in draft).

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of items to return

post.description: Creates a queue in draft status for a category. Published posts are auto-added. Use update endpoint to activate.

post.summary: Fetch category queues for a location
post.description: Retrieves a paginated list of all category queues for a given location, excluding any that have been marked as deleted.

get.summary: Fetch a category queue by ID
get.description: Retrieves the details of a single category queue by its unique ID. The response includes a count of posts within the queue that have errors.

put.description: Updates queue status (active/paused/deleted), time slots, or skip dates.

post.description: Returns paginated queue items. Pass sessionId to get draft items from an edit session instead of live items.

post.summary: Start or resume an edit session
post.description: Creates a draft copy of queue items for editing. Changes are staged until saved or discarded.
Applies all staged changes to the live queue and closes the edit session.
Cancels the edit session and deletes all staged changes without affecting the live queue.

post.summary: Fetch calendar view for an edit session
post.description: Retrieves a calendar preview of scheduled posts based on draft items within an edit session. This shows how posts would be scheduled if changes were saved.

post.summary: Fetch slot information for queue items
post.description: Returns paginated slot information (scheduledDateTime, isSkipped) for queue items. Pass sessionId to get slots for draft items, or omit for live items. Call this after mutations to refresh slot data.

delete.summary: Delete an item from a queue
delete.description: Deletes an item from a specific category queue.

200.description: The queue item has been successfully deleted.

put.summary: Update an item in a queue
put.description: Updates the content or variations of a specific item within a category queue.

200.description: The queue item has been successfully updated.

post.description: Returns scheduled posts from active queues within a date range. Supports filtering by categories and accounts.

delete.summary: Delete an active post and schedule the next one
delete.description: Deletes a post that is currently scheduled and automatically triggers the scheduling of the next available post in the queue.

200.description: Successfully deleted the active post and scheduled the next one.

put.summary: Reset an item in a queue
put.description: Resets a specific queue item to its original state, discarding any modifications made.

200.description: The queue item has been successfully reset.

post.description: Duplicates an existing queue item at a specified order position. Requires an active edit session.

post.summary: Create a new item in the queue
post.description: Adds a new post item to a queue. Use sessionId for edit session or directToQueue for immediate addition.

Create a top-level comment on a post (`isParentThread: true`, `parentId` = postId) or a reply to an existing comment (`isParentThread: false`, `parentId` = commentId). Per-platform content max length: Facebook 8000, Instagram 2200, Linkedin 3000, Community 8000, Tiktok 150, Bluesky 300, Youtube 10000, Threads 500.

**Optional-field platform support:**
- `attachments` — supported on **Facebook only**. Ignored on Instagram, LinkedIn, TikTok, Bluesky, Community (Community processes the field but external URLs are not rendered due to its bucket restriction).
- `mentions` — supported on **Facebook**, **LinkedIn**, and **Community** only. Ignored on Instagram, TikTok, Bluesky.
- `notifyAllGroupMembers` — supported on **Community** only. When `true`, all group members get a push/in-app notification (equivalent to an `@everyone` broadcast). Independent of the `mentions` array and of `@everyone` text in `content`. Default `false`.

Like a comment by its **Highlevel** comment ID (the `_id` returned by the list-comments endpoint — not the native platform ID).

Works for any comment level — top-level comments, replies, and replies-to-replies. **Supported platforms:** Facebook, LinkedIn, Community, TikTok, Bluesky. Instagram is not supported (passing `instagram` returns 400).

parameters.name: platform
parameters.description: Platform that supports liking / unliking comments (Instagram is not supported)

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Highlevel comment ID — the `_id` returned by the list-comments endpoint (`POST /comments/{platform}/list`). Not the native platform comment ID. Works for any comment level: top-level comments, replies, and replies-to-replies.

400.description: State conflict (already liked) or unsupported platform (Instagram)

delete.description:
Remove a like from a comment by its **Highlevel** comment ID (the `_id` returned by the list-comments endpoint — not the native platform ID).

Works for any comment level — top-level comments, replies, and replies-to-replies. **Supported platforms:** Facebook, LinkedIn, Community, TikTok, Bluesky. Instagram is not supported (passing `instagram` returns 400).

parameters.name: platform
parameters.description: Platform that supports liking / unliking comments (Instagram is not supported)

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Highlevel comment ID — the `_id` returned by the list-comments endpoint (`POST /comments/{platform}/list`). Not the native platform comment ID. Works for any comment level: top-level comments, replies, and replies-to-replies.

400.description: State conflict (not currently liked) or unsupported platform (Instagram)

post.summary: List comments for a post or thread
post.description: Paginated list of comments scoped to a post (`parentId` = postId) or a comment thread (`parentId` = commentId). Use `skip`/`limit` for pagination, `sortBy` for ordering, `originIds` to filter by connected account, and `search` for keyword search.

bearer.name: Authorization
bearer.description: Use the Access Token generated with user type as Sub-Account (OR) Private Integration Token of Sub-Account.

Location-Access.name: Authorization
Location-Access.description: Use the Access Token generated with user type as Sub-Account (OR) Private Integration Token of Sub-Account.

Agency-Access.name: Authorization
Agency-Access.description: Use the Access Token generated with user type as Agency (OR) Private Integration Token of Agency.

metadata.description: Meta data not related to User

relationshipData.description: All locations and chain related to this one

maxLocation.description: Indicates if location limit has been reached

isVerified.description: Indicates if the location is verified by Google

isConnected.description: Indicates if the location is currently connected

accountName.description: Display name of the Google Business account

type.description: Type of Google Business account

vettedState.description: Vetted state of the account by Google

maxLocation.description: Whether this is a max location

type.description: Type of the account

_id.description: MongoDB document ID of the social media account

oldId.description: Legacy account identifier for backward compatibility

locationId.description: Location ID associated with this account

type.description: Type of account (e.g., location, page, profile)

active.description: Indicates if the account is currently active

deleted.description: Indicates if the account has been deleted

type.description: type must be one of the following values: recent, all, scheduled, draft, failed, in_review, published, in_progress, pending and deleted

accounts.description: List of account Ids separated by comma as a string

skip.description: Number of records to skip for pagination

limit.description: Maximum number of records to return

postType.description: Post Type must be one of the following values: - post, story, reel

url.description: Public URL of the media file. Must be a valid, accessible HTTPS URL.

caption.description: Alt text or caption for the media. Used for accessibility and SEO.
caption.example: Team meeting discussing Q4 marketing strategy

originalUrl.description: Original media URL before any processing (watermarking/optimization). Set automatically by the system.

watermarkedUrl.description: URL of the media after watermarking. Set automatically when watermark is applied.

type.description: MIME type of the media file. See Platform Limitations Guide for platform-specific format support.

thumbnail.description:
Cover image URL for a video media item.

**Scope**
- Applies to the **first video** in `media[]`. Values supplied on subsequent video items are ignored.
- Has no effect on image-only media items.

**Response behavior**
- After the post is published, the resolved cover image is surfaced on the post-level `thumbnail` field of the response and is no longer echoed back inside `media[]`. This represents the post's primary cover image and the cover used for the first video.

**Limitations**
- Per-item thumbnails for multi-video posts are not supported. Each destination network exposes a single cover image per post for the leading video; this matches the behavior of the underlying social platform APIs.

**Format**
- Provide a publicly accessible JPEG or PNG URL. Animated formats and video URLs are not accepted.
- Recommended: an image matching the orientation of your video and ≤ 2 MB.

id.description: Unique identifier for the media item. Used for tracking and referencing.

optimizedUrl.description:
URL of the optimized/compressed media. Set automatically when media optimization is enabled.

**Enable Optimization:** Set `mediaOptimization: true` in the post request.

optimizedType.description: MIME type of the optimized media. May differ from original if format conversion occurred.

isModified.description: Flag indicating if the media was modified (watermarked, optimized, or processed).

altText.description: Alt text for accessibility. Supported on Instagram, Threads, Pinterest, Bluesky, and LinkedIn image posts (ignored for video and other platforms). Auto-truncated per platform: Pinterest 500, Instagram/Threads 1000, Bluesky 2000, LinkedIn 4086 chars.
altText.example: A sunset over the ocean with silhouetted palm trees

metaImage.description:
Preview image URL for the shared link.

**Best Practices:**
- Use high-quality images (1200x630px recommended)
- Ensure the image is publicly accessible

**Auto-fetch:** Use the Get Metatags API to fetch OG data from a URL.

metaLink.description: URL of the webpage being shared. This is the destination when users click the link preview.

ogTitle.description:
Custom title for the link preview. Overrides the page's og:title meta tag.

**Tip:** Keep titles concise (50-60 characters) for best display across platforms.
ogTitle.example: How to Double Your Social Media Engagement

ogDescription.description:
Custom description for the link preview. Overrides the page's og:description meta tag.

**Tip:** Keep descriptions under 155 characters for optimal display.
ogDescription.example: Learn proven strategies to boost your social media engagement and grow your audience.

approver.description:
User ID of the designated approver.

**Note:** The approver will receive a notification when a post is submitted for review.

requesterNote.description: Note from the post creator to the approver explaining the post or requesting specific feedback.
requesterNote.example: Please review the messaging for our Q4 campaign launch. Let me know if the CTA needs adjustment.

approverNote.description: Note from the approver to the post creator with feedback or approval comments.
approverNote.example: Approved! Great content. Consider adding more hashtags for visibility.

approvalStatus.description:
Current approval status of the post.

**Available Values:**
- `pending` - Awaiting approver review
- `approved` - Approved and ready for publishing
- `rejected` - Rejected by approver (needs revision)
- `not_required` - No approval workflow needed

privacyLevel.description:
Privacy level controlling who can view the video.

**Available Values:**
- `PUBLIC_TO_EVERYONE` - Anyone can view (default)
- `MUTUAL_FOLLOW_FRIENDS` - Only mutual followers can view
- `SELF_ONLY` - Only you can view (private)

**Note:** If set to `SELF_ONLY`, `videoDisclosure` must be `false`.

promoteOtherBrand.description:
Indicates if the video promotes a third-party brand or product.

**Required:** Must be `true` if `videoDisclosure` is enabled and you're promoting another brand.

enableComment.description: Allow users to comment on the video. Default is determined by account settings.

enableDuet.description:
Allow users to create Duet videos with your content.

**Duet:** Side-by-side video featuring your content and the creator's reaction/addition.

enableStitch.description:
Allow users to create Stitch videos with your content.

**Stitch:** Clips up to 5 seconds of your video that creators can use in their own videos.

videoDisclosure.description:
Enable branded content disclosure. Required when video is promotional content.

**Validations:**
- Cannot be `true` if `privacyLevel` is `SELF_ONLY`
- If enabled, at least one of `promoteYourBrand` or `promoteOtherBrand` must be `true`

promoteYourBrand.description:
Indicates if the video promotes your own brand or product.

**Required:** Must be `true` if `videoDisclosure` is enabled and you're promoting your own brand.

gmbEventType.description:
Google My Business post type.

**Available Types:**
- `STANDARD` - Regular update post (What's New)
- `EVENT` - Event announcement with dates and title
- `OFFER` - Promotional offer with coupon and redemption details

**Required fields by type:**
- STANDARD: None additional
- EVENT: `title`, `startDate`, `endDate`
- OFFER: `offerTitle`, `startDate`, `endDate`, `termsConditions`, `couponCode`, `redeemOnlineUrl`

title.description:
Event title. Required when `gmbEventType` is `EVENT`.

**Max length:** 58 characters

termsConditions.description: URL to terms and conditions page. Required for OFFER posts.

url.description:
Call-to-action URL. Required when `actionType` is set (except `none` and `call`).

**Required for:** STANDARD and EVENT posts with actionType other than `none` or `call`.

couponCode.description: Promotional coupon code. Required for OFFER posts.

redeemOnlineUrl.description: URL where customers can redeem the offer online. Required for OFFER posts.

actionType.description:
Call-to-action button type for the post.

**Available Actions:**
- `none` - No action button
- `order` - Order online
- `book` - Book appointment
- `shop` - Shop now
- `learn_more` - Learn more
- `call` - Call now (no URL required)
- `sign_up` - Sign up

**Note:** All actions except `none` and `call` require a `url`.

replyTo.description:
Bluesky AT Protocol URI of a post to reply to.

**Format:** `at://did:plc:{user-id}/app.bsky.feed.post/{post-id}`

**Use Case:** Create a reply thread to an existing Bluesky post.

quotePost.description:
Bluesky AT Protocol URI of a post to quote.

**Format:** `at://did:plc:{user-id}/app.bsky.feed.post/{post-id}`

**Use Case:** Quote-post another user's post with your commentary.

language.description:
ISO 639-1 language code for the post content.

**Examples:** `en` (English), `es` (Spanish), `fr` (French), `de` (German)

externalLink.description: External URL to embed as a link card in the post.

externalLinkTitle.description: Title for the external link card. Displayed prominently in the embed.
externalLinkTitle.example: 10 Tips for Better Social Media Marketing

externalLinkDescription.description: Description for the external link card. Brief summary displayed below the title.
externalLinkDescription.example: Learn how to improve your social media presence with these proven strategies.

text.description: Text describing the option. Max length: 30 characters.

duration.description: Duration the poll stays open for votes.

question.description: Question for the poll. Max length: 140 characters.

options.description: Poll options. Minimum 2, maximum 4. Each option text max 30 characters. Option texts must be unique.

pdfTitle.description:
Title for the PDF document carousel. Displayed as the document name on LinkedIn.

**Max length:** 100 characters

**Tip:** Use a descriptive title that explains the document content.

postAsPdf.description:
Post images as a PDF document carousel.

**Limits:**
- Max 300 pages/images
- Max PDF size: 100 MB

poll.description:
Publish a LinkedIn poll post.

**Required fields when `poll` is supplied:**
- `question` (max 140 characters)
- `options`: 2 to 4 entries, each `text` ≤ 30 characters; option texts must be unique
- `settings.duration`: one of `ONE_DAY`, `THREE_DAYS`, `SEVEN_DAYS`, `FOURTEEN_DAYS`

**LinkedIn restrictions:**
- Polls cannot be edited after publishing.
- A poll post cannot include `media` or a meta-link preview.

PinterestBoardSelection.description: Per-account Pinterest board selection. Each entry binds one connected Pinterest account to a list of board IDs the pin should publish to. Each selected board produces an independent child post tracked separately for success/failure.

accountId.description: Connected Pinterest account ID. Must match one of the accounts referenced in the post's `userIds`.

boards.description: Pinterest board IDs to publish to on this account. Each board produces an independent child post. Capped at 25 boards per account.

title.description:
Pin title displayed on Pinterest.

**Max length:** 100 characters

**Best Practices:**
- Include relevant keywords
- Be descriptive and engaging

link.description:
Destination URL for the pin. Users clicking the pin will be directed to this URL.

**Max length:** 2048 characters

**Best Practices:**
- Use direct links to relevant content
- Track with UTM parameters for analytics

boardIds.description:
**DEPRECATED — use `pinterestBoards` instead.** Will be removed on July 31, 2026.

Legacy mapping of Pinterest account OAuth IDs to a single board ID:
`{ accountOAuthId: "boardId" }`

For multi-board posting, use `pinterestBoards`.

pinterestBoards.description:
Per-account Pinterest board selection. Each entry binds one connected Pinterest account to a list of boards on that account. Each board produces an independent child post tracked separately for success/failure. Capped at 25 boards per account.

When supplied, this field takes precedence over the deprecated `boardIds` field.

type.description:
Facebook post format type.

**Available Types:**
- `post` - Standard feed post (images, videos, text)
- `story` - 24-hour temporary story
- `reel` - Short-form vertical video

**Restrictions:**
- Reels: Require exactly 1 video, not supported on Groups
- Stories: Captions not supported

textFormatPresetId.description:
Facebook background preset ID for text-only feed posts. **Facebook `post` only** — not `story` or `reel`. Ignored when media is attached; `metaLink` is omitted on publish.

**Validations** — request returns `400` if violated:
- Must be a valid preset ID from the [Facebook text background preset reference](https://help.leadconnectorhq.com/support/solutions/articles/155000008005-facebook-text-background-posts-preset-reference). Empty strings, whitespace-only values, and `null` are rejected.
- When set, `summary` must be 130 characters or fewer.

type.description:
Instagram post format type.

**Available Types:**
- `post` - Standard feed post (images, videos, carousels)
- `story` - 24-hour temporary story
- `reel` - Short-form vertical video (up to 90 seconds)

**Restrictions:**
- Media is REQUIRED for all Instagram posts
- Reels: Require exactly 1 video
- Stories: Captions not supported, JPEG only for images

collaborators.description: Object mapping account IDs to arrays of associated usernames for collaboration. Only allowed for type "post" and "reels"

showOnFeed.description:
Show Reel on profile grid/feed.

**✅ Applies to:** Reels only

- `true` - Reel appears on your profile grid
- `false` - Reel only appears in Reels tab

publishViaPushNotification.description: Send Instagram Story via Push  Notification instead of direct posting. Only applicable for Story type.

publisherNote.description: Note to the publisher for manual posting guidance. Only used when publishViaPushNotification is true.
publisherNote.example: When publishing, add swipe up link to the landing page so that we can direct them to the sales page

title.description:
Video title displayed on YouTube.

**Max length:** 100 characters

**Best Practices:**
- Include relevant keywords
- Be descriptive but concise
- Avoid clickbait
title.example: How to Build a Successful Marketing Strategy in 2024

privacyLevel.description:
Video visibility/privacy setting.

**Available Values:**
- `public` - Anyone can search and view
- `unlisted` - Only people with the link can view
- `private` - Only you can view

type.description:
YouTube video format type.

**Available Types:**
- `video` - Standard YouTube video (landscape, any duration)
- `short` - YouTube Shorts (vertical, up to 60 seconds)

**Required field.**

_id.description: MongoDB document ID of the post

source.description: source must be one of the following values: composer, recurring, csv

platform.description: platform must be one of the following values: google, facebook, instagram, linkedin, twitter, tiktok

thumbnail.description:
Post-level cover image (thumbnail) URL.

For posts that contain a video, this is the resolved cover image used for the first video in `media[]`. It is set automatically from the `thumbnail` provided on the first video media item at create/edit time, or auto-generated from the video when one is not supplied. Per-item thumbnails inside `media[]` are not retained on the response — the primary cover image lives on this field.

createdAt.description: Date when the post was created

updatedAt.description: Date when the post was last updated

publishedAt.description: Date when the post was published

media.description:
Post Media Data 
 The limitations of media as per the platforms is provided through the reference link in API description

example.caption: Sample caption
example.altText: A sunset over the ocean with silhouetted palm trees

status.description: Status must be one of the following values: in_progress, draft, failed, published, scheduled, in_review, notification_sent, deleted

createdBy.description: User ID who created the post

type.description: Post Type must be one of the following values: - post, story, reel

mediaOptimization.description: Pass this parameter to optimize the image media

insights.description: Aggregated engagement metrics for the published post. Populated asynchronously by the insights sync workers for supported platforms (Facebook, Instagram, LinkedIn, YouTube). Absent on posts that have not been synced yet or on platforms where insights are not supported.

PostInsightsSchema.description: Aggregated engagement metrics for a published post.

like.description: Total number of likes (or platform-equivalent reactions) on the post.

share.description: Total number of shares/reposts of the post.

comment.description: Total number of comments on the post.

approver.description:
User ID of the designated approver.

**Note:** The approver will receive a notification when a post is submitted for review.

requesterNote.description: Note from the post creator to the approver explaining the post or requesting specific feedback.
requesterNote.example: Please review the messaging for our Q4 campaign launch. Let me know if the CTA needs adjustment.

approverNote.description: Note from the approver to the post creator with feedback or approval comments.
approverNote.example: Approved! Great content. Consider adding more hashtags for visibility.

approvalStatus.description:
Current approval status of the post.

**Available Values:**
- `pending` - Awaiting approver review
- `approved` - Approved and ready for publishing
- `rejected` - Rejected by approver (needs revision)
- `not_required` - No approval workflow needed

accountIds.description:
Account IDs for the post. Each account ID identifies a connected social media account.

**Get IDs from:** [Get Accounts API](./get-account) — use the `id` field from each account.

**Validations:**
- Required for non-draft posts
- Must be a non-empty array
- All account IDs must be valid connected accounts for the location

summary.description:
Post content/caption text. Character limits vary by platform.

**Custom Values & Hashtags:**
- You can include custom values/variables in the content (e.g., `{{contact.name}}`)
- Hashtags: Use `#hashtag` format. Instagram allows max 30 hashtags.
- Mentions: Use platform-specific mention format (see `mentions` field for structured mentions)

**Validations:**
- Instagram/Facebook Story: Caption NOT supported for direct publishing
- Facebook, LinkedIn, GMB: Content OR media is required (at least one)
- Content is automatically trimmed to platform limits

**Reference:** [Platform Limitations Guide](https://help.leadconnectorhq.com/support/solutions/articles/48001240003-social-planner-image-video-content-and-api-limitations)
summary.example: Hello World! Check out our latest updates. #social #marketing

media.description:
Post Media Data 
 The limitations of media as per the platforms is provided through the reference link in API description

example.caption: Sample caption
example.altText: A sunset over the ocean with silhouetted palm trees

status.description:
Post status indicating the current state of the post.

**Available Status Values:**
- `draft` - Post saved as draft, not yet ready for publishing
- `scheduled` - Post scheduled for future publishing (requires `scheduleDate`)
- `in_review` - Post pending approval (requires `scheduleDate` and `postApprovalDetails`)
- `published` - Post has been published
- `in_progress` - Post is currently being processed
- `pending` - Post is awaiting platform processing for Instagram media container creation
- `failed` - Post publishing failed
- `notification_sent` - Story notification sent (for manual story posting)
- `deleted` - Post has been deleted

**Validations:**
- `scheduled` or `in_review` status requires `scheduleDate` to be set
- Draft posts skip most validations (accountIds, media requirements)

selectedBestTime.description: Selected Best Time slot for scheduling

createdBy.description:
User ID of the creator who is creating/managing the post. Must be a valid MongoDB ObjectId.

**Get User IDs from:** [Get User API](../users/get-user) — use the `id` field from the user object.

**Validation:** Must be a valid MongoDB ObjectId.

followUpComment.description:
Follow-up comment to be posted immediately after the main post is published.

**Supported Platforms:** Facebook, Instagram, LinkedIn, YouTube

**NOT Supported:** TikTok, Google My Business (GMB), Pinterest

**Use Case:** Great for adding hashtags, additional context, or engagement prompts without cluttering the main post.

- Follow-up comment is automatically trimmed to platform limits

**Reference:** [Platform Limitations Guide](https://help.leadconnectorhq.com/support/solutions/articles/48001240003-social-planner-image-video-content-and-api-limitations)
followUpComment.example: What do you think? Let us know in the comments!

type.description:
Type of post to create. Determines the format and platform requirements.

**Available Types:**
- `post` - Standard feed post (all platforms)
- `story` - Temporary 24-hour story (Instagram, Facebook)
- `reel` - Short-form video content (Instagram, Facebook, TikTok, YouTube)

**Customize Per Platform:**
You can specify different content/types per platform using `facebookPostDetails.type`, `instagramPostDetails.type`, etc.

**Validations:**
- Reels require exactly 1 video
- Stories: Caption not supported for Instagram/Facebook
- Facebook Groups do not support Reels

scheduleTimeUpdated.description: Flag indicating if the schedule datetime was manually updated. Used for tracking rescheduled posts.

tags.description:
Array of Tag IDs to associate with the post for organization and filtering.

**Get Tag IDs from:** [Get Tags API](./social-planner/get-tags-location-id) — use the `_id` field from each tag.

**Validation:** All IDs must be valid MongoDB ObjectIds.

categoryId.description:
Category ID to organize the post. Categories help group related posts.

**Get Category IDs from:** [Get Categories API](./social-planner/get-categories-location-id) — use the `_id` field.

**Validation:** Must be a valid MongoDB ObjectId.

applyWatermark.description:
Apply watermark to media in this post.

**Note:** Watermarks are applied to images only. Videos are not watermarked.

userId.description: User ID of the user creating/managing the post. Required for OAuth channel posts (non-draft).

linkedinPostDetails.description:
LinkedIn-specific post configuration.

**Key Fields:**
- `postAsPdf`: Set to `true` to post images as a PDF carousel document
- `pdfTitle`: Title for the PDF document (max 100 characters)

**Limits:**
- Max 9 images/videos for regular posts
- Max 300 pages for PDF carousel
- Max PDF size: 100 MB

**Reference:** [Platform Limitations Guide](https://help.leadconnectorhq.com/support/solutions/articles/48001240003-social-planner-image-video-content-and-api-limitations)

pinterestPostDetails.description:
Pinterest-specific post configuration. Required when posting to Pinterest accounts.

**Required Fields:**
- `boardIds`: Object mapping account OAuth IDs to Pinterest board IDs

**Optional Fields:**
- `title`: Pin title (max 100 characters)
- `link`: Destination URL for the pin (max 2048 characters)

**Get Board IDs:** Use the Pinterest boards API or retrieve from connected account details.

**Limits:**
- Max 1 image/video per pin
- Caption max 800 characters

**Reference:** [Platform Limitations Guide](https://help.leadconnectorhq.com/support/solutions/articles/48001240003-social-planner-image-video-content-and-api-limitations)

facebookPostDetails.description:
Facebook-specific post configuration.

**Key Fields:**
- `type`: Post type (`post`, `story`, `reel`)

**Restrictions:**
- Facebook Groups do NOT support Reels
- Reels require exactly 1 video
- Stories do not support captions

**Reference:** [Platform Limitations Guide](https://help.leadconnectorhq.com/support/solutions/articles/48001240003-social-planner-image-video-content-and-api-limitations)

instagramPostDetails.description:
Instagram-specific post configuration.

**Key Fields:**
- `type`: Post type (`post`, `story`, `reel`)
- `collaborators`: Map of account IDs to Instagram usernames for collaboration invites (max 3 per account)
- `showOnFeed`: Show reel on profile feed (for reels)

**Collaborators Structure:**
```json
{ "accountId": ["username1", "username2"] }
```
Where `accountId` is from [Get Accounts API](./get-account) and usernames are Instagram handles without @.

**Restrictions:**
- Media is REQUIRED for all Instagram posts
- Max 30 hashtags allowed in caption
- Stories do not support captions
- Collaborators: Posts/Reels only (NOT Stories)
- Reels require exactly 1 video

**Reference:** [Platform Limitations Guide](https://help.leadconnectorhq.com/support/solutions/articles/48001240003-social-planner-image-video-content-and-api-limitations)

youtubePostDetails.description:
YouTube-specific post configuration.

**Key Fields:**
- `title`: Video title (max 100 characters)
- `type`: Video type (`video` for regular videos, `short` for YouTube Shorts)
- `privacyLevel`: Video visibility (`private`, `public`, `unlisted`)

**Limits:**
- Max 1 video per post
- Caption (description) max 5,000 characters

**Requirements:**
- Video is REQUIRED for YouTube posts
- `type` field is required

status.description:
Post status indicating the current state of the post.

**Available Status Values:**
- `draft` - Post saved as draft, not yet ready for publishing
- `scheduled` - Post scheduled for future publishing (requires `scheduleDate`)
- `in_review` - Post pending approval (requires `scheduleDate` and `postApprovalDetails`)
- `published` - Post has been published
- `in_progress` - Post is currently being processed
- `pending` - Post is awaiting platform processing for Instagram media container creation
- `failed` - Post publishing failed
- `notification_sent` - Story notification sent (for manual story posting)
- `deleted` - Post has been deleted

**Validations:**
- `scheduled` or `in_review` status requires `scheduleDate` to be set
- Draft posts skip most validations (accountIds, media requirements)

tags.description:
Array of Tag IDs to associate with the post for organization and filtering.

**Get Tag IDs from:** [Get Tags API](./social-planner/get-tags-location-id) — use the `_id` field from each tag.

**Validation:** All IDs must be valid MongoDB ObjectIds.

categoryId.description:
Category ID to organize the post. Categories help group related posts.

**Get Category IDs from:** [Get Categories API](./social-planner/get-categories-location-id) — use the `_id` field.

**Validation:** Must be a valid MongoDB ObjectId.

applyWatermark.description:
Apply watermark to media in this post.

**Note:** Watermarks are applied to images only. Videos are not watermarked.

metaLink.description: The canonical URL of the content for link preview.

metaImage.description: URL of the image to display in link preview.

ogTitle.description: Title to display in link preview.
ogTitle.example: Check out our latest blog post!

content.description: The text content of the variation.
content.example: Check out our latest blog post! #marketing #socialmedia

mentions.description: Platform-specific mentions within the content (e.g., @username references).

ogTags.description: Open Graph tags for link previews.

newOrder.description:
New order value or position keyword (cyclic-aware). Accepts:
- A number: explicit order value calculated by FE as midpoint between adjacent items
- "top": place at cyclic top (first to be scheduled next)
- "bottom": place at cyclic bottom (last to be scheduled)

For positions between items, FE calculates: Math.floor((prevItem.order + nextItem.order) / 2)

_id.description: MongoDB document ID of the post

source.description: source must be one of the following values: composer, recurring, csv

createdAt.description: Date when the post was created

updatedAt.description: Date when the post was last updated

publishedAt.description: Date when the post was published

thumbnail.description:
Post-level cover image (thumbnail) URL.

For posts that contain a video, this is the resolved cover image used for the first video in `media[]`. It is set automatically from the `thumbnail` provided on the first video media item at create/edit time, or auto-generated from the video when one is not supplied. Per-item thumbnails inside `media[]` are not retained on the response — the primary cover image lives on this field.

media.description:
Post Media Data 
 The limitations of media as per the platforms is provided through the reference link in API description

example.caption: Sample caption
example.altText: A sunset over the ocean with silhouetted palm trees

status.description: Status must be one of the following values: in_progress, draft, failed, published, scheduled, in_review, notification_sent, deleted

createdBy.description: User ID who created the post

type.description: Post Type must be one of the following values: - post, story, reel

mediaOptimization.description: Pass this parameter to optimize the image media

order.description: The order of the item in the queue.

variations.description: A list of content variations for the post.

primaryImage.description: The primary image URL for the post.

queueId.description: The ID of the queue this post belongs to

postId.description: The ID of the original post, if any.

modifiedPostPayload.description: The formatted post data modified from original post.

parentPostId.description: The ID of the parent post before splitting to individual social posts.

errors.description: List of errors associated with the queue item. Possible values: INVALID_USER_ID, PIXABAY_MEDIA.

currentVariation.description: The index of the current variation being used for this post

deleted.description: Indicates if the item is deleted

message.description: A message indicating the result of the operation.

updatedSlots.description: Updated slot information for items affected by reorder operation

totalPostsChanged.description: Number of unique posts that had their slots changed

accountIds.description: Filter by Account IDs. If not provided or empty, returns all posts.

scheduledDateTime.description: The date and time the post is scheduled to be published

post.description: The formatted post data.

queueItemId.description: The unique identifier of the queue item.

queueId.description: The ID of the queue this post belongs to

order.description: The order of the item in the queue.

variations.description: A list of content variations for the post.

primaryImage.description: The primary image URL for the post.

errors.description: List of errors associated with the queue item. Possible values: INVALID_USER_ID, PIXABAY_MEDIA.

category.description: The category associated with this post

currentVariation.description: The index of the current variation being used for this post

timezone.description: The timezone in which the post is scheduled

total.description: Total number of scheduled posts returned

timezone.description: The timezone used for scheduling, e.g., "Asia/Calcutta"

message.example: Current post deleted and next post scheduled successfully
message.description: A message indicating the result of the operation.

_id.description: The unique identifier of the queue item.

order.description: The order of the item in the queue.

variations.description: A list of content variations for the post.

primaryImage.description: The primary image URL for the post.

postId.description: The ID of the original post, if any.

post.description: The formatted post data.

errors.description: List of errors associated with the queue item. Possible values: INVALID_USER_ID, PIXABAY_MEDIA.

scheduledDateTime.description: The calculated date/time when this item is scheduled to be posted

scheduledVariationIndex.description: The variation index that will be used when this item is posted

isSkipped.description: Indicates if this time slot is skipped and the post will not be published at this time

currentVariation.description: The index of the current variation being used for this post

message.description: A message indicating the result of the operation. Examples: "Queue item reset successfully", "Dummy queue item deleted successfully".

queueItem.description: The reset queue item, including its current variation.

order.description: Order for the cloned item (typically between source and next item)

message.description: A message indicating the result of the operation.

order.description:
Order for the new item in the queue (cyclic-aware). Accepts:
- A number: explicit order value calculated by FE as midpoint between adjacent items
- "top": place at cyclic top (first to be scheduled next)
- "bottom": place at cyclic bottom (last to be scheduled)

For positions between items, FE calculates: Math.floor((prevItem.order + nextItem.order) / 2)
Defaults to end if not provided. Note: This field is ignored when directToQueue is true -
the order will be automatically calculated based on the queue's prioritizeNewContent setting.

primaryImage.description: Primary media URL (image) for the post. Falls back to modifiedPostPayload.primaryImage if not set.

directToQueue.description: When true, creates the queue item directly without requiring an edit session, even for active/paused queues. The order field is ignored and the item position is determined by the queue's prioritizeNewContent setting: if true, the item is added to the top of the queue; if false, it is added to the bottom.

message.description: A message indicating the result of the operation.

parentId.description: For top-level comments (`isParentThread: true`): pass the post ID returned by the posts API. For replies (`isParentThread: false`): pass the parent comment ID returned by the list-comments API. In both cases this must be a valid 24-character Highlevel ID — not the native platform ID.

isParentThread.description: Set `true` to create a top-level comment on a post (parentId = post ID). Set `false` to create a reply to an existing comment (parentId = comment ID).

content.description: Content of the comment. Per-platform max length: Facebook 8000, Instagram 2200, Linkedin 3000, Community 8000, Tiktok 150, Bluesky 300, Youtube 10000, Threads 500.

attachments.description: Attachments for the comment (max 1 image). **Supported on:** Facebook only. **Not supported on:** Instagram, LinkedIn, TikTok, Bluesky, Community — the field is accepted by the API but the attachment will not appear on the comment. (Community processes the field server-side, but external URLs are not rendered due to its bucket restriction.)

mentions.description: Mentions for the comment. **Supported on:** Facebook, LinkedIn, Community. **Ignored on:** Instagram, TikTok, Bluesky — the field is accepted but mentions are not rendered on these platforms.

notifyAllGroupMembers.description:
When `true`, all members of the Community group receive a push/in-app notification about this comment — equivalent to an `@everyone` broadcast.

**Supported on:** Community only. Ignored on all other platforms (the field is accepted but no notification is sent).

**Independent of the `mentions` array** — you do not need to add an `@everyone` entry to `mentions` for this to take effect. Conversely, putting the literal text `@everyone` in `content` does **not** by itself trigger notifications; only this flag does.

Defaults to `false` (no broadcast notification). Use `true` only when the comment is genuinely intended for every member of the group — overuse may cause members to mute the group.

fromDate.description: Start of the published-date window (ISO 8601). If provided, `toDate` is also required, and `fromDate` must be ≤ `toDate`. Omit both to disable date filtering.

toDate.description: End of the published-date window (ISO 8601). If provided, `fromDate` is also required.

originIds.description: Origin IDs of connected accounts to filter by

sortBy.description: Sort by top comments or latest comments

skip.description: Pagination offset — number of comments to skip (zero-based). Must be ≥ 0.

limit.description: Pagination page size — number of comments to return. Must be between 1 and 100.

parentId.description: Parent ID — pass the Highlevel post ID (for replies under a specific post) or the Highlevel comment ID (for replies under a specific comment). Omit to list all top-level comments for the location filtered by `originIds`. Must be a valid 24-character Highlevel ID, not the native platform ID.

type.description: Type of the attachment

slug.description: Mention slug for community profile link

platform.description: Platform the comment was posted on

platformParentId.description: Native platform parent ID (the post or comment this is a reply to)

originId.description: Connected account / page ID on the native platform

isParentThread.description: True if this comment is a top-level comment on the post; false if it is a reply to another comment

isPost.description: True if this record represents the root post (not a comment)

content.description: Comment content. May be empty or missing for attachment-only comments.

author.description: Author of the comment. May be partial or missing for some sync paths.

likeCount.description: Number of likes on the comment

reactionCount.description: Number of reactions on the comment

replyCount.description: Number of replies to the comment

shareCount.description: Number of shares of the comment

repostCount.description: Number of reposts of the comment (platform-specific)

previewLink.description: Direct link to view the comment on the native platform

isRead.description: Whether the comment has been read

publishedAt.description: Time the comment was published on the native platform. May be missing for legacy or webhook-synced records.

createdAt.description: Time the comment record was created in Highlevel

updatedAt.description: Time the comment record was last updated in Highlevel

totalUnread.description: Total unread comments matching the query

hasMore.description: True if more pages exist beyond this batch

type.description: Attachment MIME type or platform-specific type

videoUrl.description: Video URL (when attachment is a video)
